title: Flying Bantha Squadron
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Bantha}}
{{Youmay|the Rebel Alliance fighter squadron|a [[Flying Bantha|modified skiff]] in "[[Force Fiction]]"}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:Arbra_TIEs.jpg]]
|name=Flying Bantha Squadron

{{Quote|This is Commander Skywalker! Code phrase—'Flying Banthas never sleep!'|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel60">[[Star Wars (1977) 60|''Star Wars'' (1977) 60]]</ref>}}
'''Flying Bantha Squadron''' was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] unit formed in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] to prevent the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] from utilizing a natural [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] communications amplifier called the [[teezl]] to turn the tide of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Under the leadership of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Flying Bantha consisted of four [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] pulled from the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], flying refitted Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] in order to blend into the massive [[Secret armada|armada]] transporting the teezl. The Flying Banthas' gambit worked, as the small unit managed to confuse the Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] and [[Battle of the secret armada|reach their target]], with Skywalker firing the decisive shot that destroyed the teezl.

==History==
{{Quote|A direct assault would be madness. Which is why we got ahold of these fighters. If we can infiltrate the armada, we might be able to destroy it from within.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel60" />}}
The four-person unit known as Flying [[Bantha/Legends|Bantha]] [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]] was formed by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], at the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], in response to reports that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had formed a massive [[secret armada]] in order to transport an immensely powerful organic [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] communications amplifier known as the [[teezl]] to the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. Fearing that the teezl's installation at the heart of the Imperial war machine would spell certain doom for the Rebels, and with the armada's size making a full-on attack inadvisable, the Alliance instead put together a plot—dubbed [[Operation Earplug]]—<ref name="arbra">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030626arbra4|series=Arbra: Sanctuary In the Storm|text=Part 4: Rebels in Disguise}}</ref> to infiltrate the Imperial force through subterfuge. As such, Flying Bantha Squadron consisted of four reconstructed and modified Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] acquired through a deal with black market arms dealer [[Orion Ferret]].<ref name="marvel59">[[Star Wars (1977) 59|''Star Wars'' (1977) 59]]</ref>

[[File:Flagship_explode.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Skywalker fires the killing blow that destroys the teezl.]]
The four [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were returned to the Alliance's [[Haven Base|main base]] at [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]] and extensively refitted under the oversight of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Hanc Thorben]].<ref name="arbra" /> To fly the fighters, the Alliance pulled four members of the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]—<ref name="negtc">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]], [[Alph (Rogue)|Alph]], and Captain Thorben.<ref name="marvel61" /> The newly formed Flying Bantha Squadron's first [[Skirmish on Spindrift|sortie]] came at the isolated Imperial outpost of [[Spindrift]], and served two purposes: not only was it a proof of concept that the disguised TIEs could pass muster, but it also acquired the route coordinates for the teezl's convoy.<ref name="marvel60" />

From there, the stage was set for the Flying Banthas to [[Battle of the secret armada|strike directly]] at the teezl itself. The Flying Banthas acquired the passcodes to enter the armada's formation after trapping, capturing and interrogating a scout pilot named [[Milnn]], and once in position they surprised the armada from within with a sudden attack. Completely confused by the sight of TIE fighters attacking other Imperial ships, a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] commander named [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Voss (Imperial officer)|Voss]] ordered his men to fire indiscriminately, plunging the arena into pure chaos. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mils Giel]] attempted to counter the Rebels' tactics by ordering the teezl to jam all frequencies except for the Imperial war band, which removed the Rebels' ability to communicate and tell friend from foe. Alph was killed during the run on Giel's [[Helmsman (battlecruiser)|flagship]], where the teezl's transport cage was located, while Thorben was forced to disengage after his fighter took damage. With Brie also unaccounted for, that left Skywalker alone to make a run on Giel's ship—relying on his connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Skywalker reached his target and destroyed the teezl with his final full-power laser blast, taking much of Giel's ship with it.<ref name="marvel61" />

Although Flying Bantha Squadron's mission was successful, only Skywalker and Thorben made it back to Arbra. Skywalker found himself disgraced upon his return, after an investigation into the records from the battle found that he had inadvertently destroyed Brie's TIE fighter immediately before striking at Giel's flagship.<ref name="marvel61" /> However, the Rebels ultimately discovered that Brie was in fact an Imperial double agent, and cleared Skywalker's name.<ref name="marvel63">[[Star Wars (1977) 63|''Star Wars'' (1977) 63]]</ref>

==Organization==
{{Quote|W-we let those TIE fighters through because they were 'friendlies,' Major! Wh-why are they opening fire?|[[Gerdy]]|<ref name="marvel60" />}}
[[File:Skirmish_on_Spindrift.jpg|thumb|right|250px|On Spindrift, Flying Bantha Squadron proved their subterfuge could work in confusing Imperial enemies.]]
Directed by flight commander Luke Skywalker,<ref name="marvel60" /> Flying Bantha Squadron was pulled from the elite Rogue Squadron,<ref name="negtc" /> forming a crack team that the Rebel Alliance hoped would be up to the challenge of defusing what it saw to be an existential threat to the war effort. In Skywalker, the squadron was under the command of one of the Rebels' most auspicious heroes and an acclaimed flight commander, while his close friend Shira Brie held high acclaim for her own flying skills and fighting prowess. Captain Hanc Thorben,<ref name="marvel61" /> also a pilot in the squadron, oversaw the technical aspects of the fighter group—his modifications to the Rebels' TIE fighters<ref name="arbra" /> allowed the four-person squadron to successfully take on the largest armada in Imperial history based solely off misdirection and subterfuge. As the Empire did not expect Rebels to be flying their distinctive TIE starfighters, Flying Bantha Squadron had the element of surprise in every sortie it flew, and used it to productive ends.<ref name="marvel61" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|While they may not be X-wings, they should do the job.|Hanc Thorben|<ref name="arbra" />}}
[[File:Battle_Giels_armada.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Flying Bantha Squadron's TIEs in battle.]]
Flying Bantha Squadron used four refitted TIE fighters originally reconstructed from parts salvaged from battlefields by weapons dealer Orion Ferret. Once in Rebel hands, the fighters received significant refits from a team of [[Technician/Legends|tech specialists]] led by Captain Thorben. This included the addition of a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] and extra [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]], none of which were standard features of a typical TIE fighter. In lieu of the [[torpedo launcher]] module Thorben originally considered, the Rebels instead rewired the fighters' [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] to fire only six much higher-power shots than usual,<ref name="arbra" /> which proved to be more than enough to obliterate the teezl's holding chamber.<ref name="marvel61" /> The fighters' [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpits]] were also modified to have ejector seating, as part of the process of reshaping the area to interface with Rebel flight gear,<ref name="arbra" /> while installed [[Subspace transceiver/Legends|transceivers]] allowed them to tell friendly and enemy TIEs apart.<ref name="marvel61" />

==Members==
===Luke Skywalker===
[[File:LukeSkywalker-RotJAVA.png|thumb|right|150px|Ace Rebel pilot Luke Skywalker, flight commander of Flying Bantha Squadron.]]
{{Quote|Don't worry, Luke. We still have the best flight commander in the galaxy!|Shira Brie|<ref name="marvel60" />}}
The flight commander for Flying Bantha Squadron,<ref name="marvel60" /> Luke Skywalker had already won renown as one of the Rebels' most talented pilots and greatest heroes, as the man responsible for the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] at the tide-turning [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="anh">{{Film|IV}}</ref> three years before. Skywalker had the admiration and respect of the rest of his group, and was the unquestioned leader of Flying Bantha Squadron throughout the entirety of Operation Earplug. His efforts ended in success and the destruction of the teezl—much as he had at [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]], Skywalker leaned on his affinity for the Force to guide his senses and deliver the winning blow.<ref name="marvel61" />

===Shira Brie===
{{Quote|Y'know, with a few more like her, we'd have this war won!|[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]|<ref name="marvel61" />}}
[[File:ShiraBrie-CSWE.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Shira Brie, crack pilot and secret Imperial agent.]]
Regarded as a major rising star in the Rebel Alliance and one of its most promising young soldiers, Shira Brie purported to come from the planet [[Shalyvane]], a survivor of Imperial atrocities in her youth.<ref name="marvel60" /> Brie rose in the ranks quickly, reaching the rank of Lieutenant and eventually [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] after the successful sortie on Spindrift, while becoming personally close with Commander Skywalker. Brie was believed dead after her starfighter was destroyed by an unwitting Skywalker during the run on Admiral Giel's flagship—Skywalker fired on Brie after the Force indicated to him that the pilot of her TIE was an enemy.<ref name="marvel61" /> He was ultimately vindicated after a [[Skirmish at Krake Data Vault|mission]] to [[Krake Data Vault]] revealed her as an Imperial agent,<ref name="marvel63" /> and Brie survived to later re-emerge as Lumiya, the "[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady of the Sith]]."<ref name="end">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref>

===Hanc Thorben===
{{Quote|I'll be lucky to get out alive, let alone reach the flagship! It's up to the others now!|Hanc Thorben|<ref name="marvel61" />}}
[[File:Thorben2.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Hanc Thorben, the man whose technical expertise made the Flying Banthas' plan possible.]]
A skilled technician, Captain Hanc Thorben played an indispensable part in the outfitting and formation of Flying Bantha Squadron, as the man who oversaw the refit and installation of their recovered TIE fighters. It was Thorben who conceived of the technical modifications that allowed the modified TIEs to successfully infiltrate the secret armada,<ref name="arbra" /> and he put the starfighters to the test by flying one himself as a member of Flying Bantha Squadron. Although Thorben sustained serious damage during the battle that forced him to withdraw from the fighting, Thorben was one of only two Flying Banthas that managed to return to Arbra at mission's end.<ref name="marvel61" />

===Alph===
{{Quote|All set, Commander!|Alph|<ref name="marvel61" />}}
The final member of Flying Bantha Squadron, Alph was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was chosen to fly one of the group's TIE fighters during the raid on the secret armada. Alph ultimately became the only member of the squadron to die during the battle, after his TIE was struck by laser fire during the Empire's wild counterattack.<ref name="marvel61" />

==Behind the scenes==
Flying Bantha Squadron appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 60|two]] [[Star Wars (1977) 61|issues]] of the original [[Marvel Comics]] ''[[Star Wars (1977)|Star Wars]]'' comic book series in [[1982]], written by [[David Michelinie]] and illustrated [[Walter Simonson]].<ref name="marvel60" /><ref name="marvel61" />

==Appearances==
